title: Question 1.7
statement_text: For fixed $k \geq 4$, is there a polynomial-time algorithm that, given a $k$-connected graph $G$ with maximal local connectivity $k$, finds a $k$-colouring of $G$, or determines that none exists?
context_text: Theorem 1.2 gives a polynomial-time algorithm for $k$-colouring when restricted to $\hat{\mathcal{C}}^k_1$ (k-connected graphs with maximal local edge-connectivity $k$). The complexity for the more general class $\hat{\mathcal{C}}^k_2$ (k-connected graphs with maximal local connectivity $k$) remains open for $k \geq 4$.
paper_title: Colouring graphs with constraints on connectivity

title: Conjecture 1.8
statement_text: For fixed $k \geq 4$, there is a polynomial-time algorithm that, given a graph $G$ with maximal local edge-connectivity $k$, finds a $k$-colouring of $G$, or determines that none exists.
context_text: The paper shows that 3-colourability is NP-complete for graphs in $\hat{\mathcal{C}}^k_1$ when $k \geq 4$, so computing the chromatic number or 3-colouring is NP-hard in this class; however, the complexity of $k$-colouring itself for graphs with maximal local edge-connectivity $k$ is unresolved, motivating this conjecture.
notes: The paper remarks that Stiebitz and Toft have recently announced a resolution of this conjecture in the affirmative [24].
paper_title: Colouring graphs with constraints on connectivity
